responseMessageContents.text:
The tunnel is unreachable. The previous ngrok tunnel died when the Expo process was killed or the URL expired. I will restart Expo with a fresh tunnel and give you the new URL.

I will NOT create a new app — this is the existing IVX project.

input.command:
ps aux | grep -E "expo|metro|ngrok" | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}' | xargs kill -9 2>/dev/null; sleep 2 && cd /home/user/rork-app/expo && rm -rf .expo node_modules/.cache /tmp/metro-* /tmp/haste-map-* /tmp/expo-* 2>/dev/null && nohup bunx expo start --tunnel --clear --port 8083 > /tmp/expo_tunnel.log 2>&1 &
echo "PID: $!"
sleep 25
echo "=== New tunnel URL ===" && curl -s --max-time 5 http://localhost:4040/api/tunnels 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys,json
d=json.load(sys.stdin)
for t in d.get('tunnels',[]):
  if 'exp.direct' in t.get('public_url',''):
    print(t.get('public_url','?'))
"

output.stdout:
PID: 52122
=== New tunnel URL ===
https://quuakf0-anonymous-8083.exp.direct
http://quuakf0-anonymous-8083.exp.direct

responseMessageContents.text:
The new Expo Go tunnel URL is:

```
exp://quuakf0-anonymous-8083.exp.direct
```

Or via `https://` fallback:
```
https://quuakf0-anonymous-8083.exp.direct
```

Try this in Expo Go. If the tunnel dies again, I can switch to a longer-lived ngrok tunnel or use the web preview instead.
